name: Solomon Islands

colors.symbolism: water — the rivers, rain, and the surrounding ocean
the land and its tropical vegetation

description: Divided diagonally from the lower hoist to the upper fly by a thin yellow stripe: a blue triangle in the upper hoist bears five white five-pointed stars arranged in two columns of two with a single star between them, and a green triangle fills the lower fly.
history: Adopted on 18 November 1977, before the country gained independence from the United Kingdom on 7 July 1978. The flag was selected from a public design competition and was modelled on a sketch by New Zealand commercial artist John Cuthbert Colquhoun.

features.symbolism: the thin yellow stripe runs diagonally from the lower hoist to the upper fly, dividing the flag into a blue upper triangle (sky and sea) and a green lower triangle (the land); the yellow stripe represents the sun

features.role: five white stars in a quincunx-like pattern
features.symbolism: originally the five main island groups of the country at the time of independence; despite the country now having nine provinces, the five stars remain
